---
title: "Performance Report"
author: "Automated Benchmark"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
engine: jupyter
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## Overview

This report automatically aggregates performance logs gathered from all tests in the repository and visualizes algorithmic execution parameters.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import glob
import json
import os
import warnings
# import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Silence warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# Find all JSONL files in the tests directory
search_path = os.path.abspath("../tests/**/*.jsonl")
jsonl_files = glob.glob(search_path, recursive=True)

print(f"**Found {len(jsonl_files)} performance log files.**")

dfs = {}
for f in jsonl_files:
    file_records = []
    with open(f, "r") as file:
        for line in file:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            try:
                data = json.loads(line)
                file_records.append(data)
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
                pass

    if file_records:
        # Pre-process records to flatten method_times, run_configs, and results_history
        processed = []
        for r in file_records:
            # Extract parameters from top_5_estimates (best fit)
            top_ests = r.pop("top_5_estimates", {})
            if isinstance(top_ests, dict) and "error" not in top_ests:
                for param, values in top_ests.items():
                    if values and isinstance(values, list):
                        r[f"param_{param}"] = values[0]

            # Flatten run_config
            r_config = r.pop("run_config", {})
            if isinstance(r_config, dict):
                for ck, cv in r_config.items():
                    r[f"config_{ck}"] = cv

            # Flatten method times and results history
            m_times = r.pop("method_times", [])
            r_hist = r.pop("results_history", [])
            
            if isinstance(m_times, list):
                method_counts = {}
                for i, mt in enumerate(m_times):
                    if isinstance(mt, dict):
                        m_name = mt.get("method")
                        m_time = mt.get("time")
                        if m_name:
                            method_counts[m_name] = method_counts.get(m_name, 0) + 1
                            suffix = (
                                f"_{method_counts[m_name]}"
                                if method_counts[m_name] > 1
                                else ""
                            )
                            # Add time
                            r[f"{m_name}{suffix}_time"] = m_time
                            
                            # Add algorithmic parameters if available in results_history
                            if i < len(r_hist) and isinstance(r_hist[i], dict):
                                for k, v in r_hist[i].items():
                                    r[f"{m_name}{suffix}_{k}"] = v
            processed.append(r)

        # Use relative path as identifier
        name = os.path.relpath(f, os.path.abspath(".."))
        df = pd.json_normalize(processed)

        # Sort by timestamp if available
        if "timestamp" in df.columns:
            df["timestamp"] = pd.to_datetime(df["timestamp"])
            df = df.sort_values("timestamp")

        dfs[name] = df

if not dfs:
    print("\n*No data available to plot.*")

else:
    from IPython.display import display, HTML, Markdown

    for name, df in dfs.items():
        display(Markdown(f"### Log: `{name}`"))
        # Organize columns: Core metrics first, then configs, then others, then parameters
        cols = list(df.columns)
        priority = ["timestamp", "loglik", "execution_time"]

        main_cols = [c for c in priority if c in cols]
        config_cols = sorted([c for c in cols if c.startswith("config_")])
        param_cols = sorted([c for c in cols if c.startswith("param_")])
        
        others = [
            c
            for c in cols
            if c not in main_cols
            and c not in config_cols
            and c not in param_cols
            and not c.startswith("results_history")
        ]

        cols_to_show = main_cols + config_cols + others + param_cols
        display(
            HTML(df[cols_to_show].to_html(classes="table table-striped table-hover"))
        )
```

<!-- ## Performance Execution Metrics

```{python}
if dfs:
    from IPython.display import Markdown, display

    for name, df in dfs.items():
        if "execution_time" in df.columns and "loglik" in df.columns:
            df_clean = df.dropna(subset=["execution_time", "loglik"])

            if not df_clean.empty:
                display(Markdown(f"#### Tradeoff for `{name}`"))
                fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
                scatter = ax.scatter(
                    df_clean["execution_time"],
                    df_clean["loglik"],
                    alpha=0.6,
                    edgecolors="w",
                    s=80,
                )
                ax.set_xlabel("Total Execution Time (seconds)")
                ax.set_ylabel("Overall Best logLik")
                ax.set_title(f"Performance Tradeoff: {name}")
                ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
                plt.tight_layout()
                plt.show()
```

## LogLik Spread Across Fits

```{python}
if dfs:
    for name, df in dfs.items():
        if 'loglik_stats.median' in df.columns:
            display(Markdown(f"#### Spread for `{name}`"))
            fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
            
            runs = range(len(df))
            has_stats = False
            
            for i, row in df.iterrows():
                try:
                    low = row['loglik_stats.min']
                    high = row['loglik_stats.max']
                    med = row['loglik_stats.median']
                    if pd.notna(low) and pd.notna(high):
                        ax.plot([i, i], [low, high], color='gray', zorder=1)
                        ax.scatter([i], [med], color='red', zorder=2, label='Median' if i == 0 else "")
                        has_stats = True
                except KeyError:
                    pass
                    
            if has_stats:
                ax.set_xticks(runs)
                ax.set_xlabel("Run Index (Chronological)")
                ax.set_ylabel("LogLik Range")
                ax.set_title(f"Spread of LogLiks: {name}")
                if i > 0: # Only show legend if we have at least one run
                     ax.legend()
                plt.tight_layout()
                plt.show()
``` -->
